skill.name: agentic-jujutsu
skill.description: Version-control workflow guidance for AI agents using Agentic Jujutsu, ReasoningBank learning, and multi-agent coordination patterns.
skill.summary: Coordinate AI agent version-control workflows with learning trajectories and conflict-aware operations.
skill.author: ruvnet

security_audit.summary: Static external-command findings are mostly true positives in documentation examples: the skill instructs users to install an external package and shows repository-mutating operations. The weak-cryptography blocker findings appear to be false positives from marketing and API text, and the network findings are documentation links only. No prompt injection attempt or confirmed malicious exfiltration was found.

medium_findings.title: External package installation and repository command execution
medium_findings.description: TRUE POSITIVE: The skill tells users to run an external npm package and includes examples that execute version-control commands. This is legitimate for the stated purpose, but it can modify repositories and should require user review before execution.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines directly show package execution and repository command examples. The context is documentation for a version-control tool, so the risk is real but not clearly malicious.

medium_findings.title: AI-recommended operations may be executed without explicit review
medium_findings.description: TRUE POSITIVE: Several examples use learned suggestions or recommended operations to drive execution paths. This can be useful automation, but it may apply unsafe repository operations if suggestions are wrong or influenced by bad history.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The examples explicitly iterate over recommended operations or pass suggestions into task execution. The skill is designed for automation, so this requires guardrails rather than a block.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URLs are documentation references
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE: The hardcoded URLs point to the npm package and GitHub documentation. No evidence found that the skill sends data to these URLs.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs appear only under related documentation. There is no network request code in the scanned skill file.

low_findings.title: Weak cryptography static findings appear to be false positives
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE: The flagged cryptography lines are marketing text, API tables, or examples naming SHA3-512 and HQC-128. No weak algorithm use was found in the scanned skill file, but users should verify security claims in the external package.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The inspected context discusses claimed security features rather than implementing cryptography. The external dependency is not present in this skill, so implementation strength cannot be confirmed here.

low_findings.title: Operation history can include sensitive command context
low_findings.description: NEEDS_REVIEW: The skill promotes automatic operation tracking and APIs that return recent operations. This can expose sensitive repository activity if logs are shared or persisted without access controls.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The documentation clearly tracks and displays operation commands, but no storage backend or sharing path is included in the scanned file. The privacy risk depends on the external package behavior.

dangerous_patterns.title: External command execution examples
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill includes command execution examples for version-control workflows. Require approval before running commands that alter repository history or publish changes.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The examples contain explicit execution calls. The commands are aligned with the skill purpose, so the concern is operational risk.

dangerous_patterns.title: External package bootstrap command
dangerous_patterns.description: The quick start uses an npm execution command. Users should inspect the package source and pin versions before running it in sensitive workspaces.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The installation command is directly documented. The scanned skill does not include the package code, so the package behavior must be reviewed separately.

content.value_statement: Multiple AI agents can create conflicting repository changes and lose useful workflow history. This skill provides Agentic Jujutsu patterns for coordination, learning trajectories, and conflict-aware version-control automation.
actual_capabilities: Explains how to start Agentic Jujutsu and create a JjWrapper workflow.
Shows how to track task trajectories and record success scores.
Describes multi-agent coordination patterns for developers, reviewers, and testers.
Documents pattern discovery, learning statistics, and operation history APIs.
Provides examples for adaptive deployment, review coordination, merge handling, and self-improving agents.
Lists validation rules for tasks, scores, operations, and context values.
limitations: Requires the external agentic-jujutsu package for actual execution.
Can run repository-changing commands when examples are adapted directly.
Cryptography and performance claims depend on the external package implementation.
Learning quality depends on accurate outcomes and enough recorded trajectories.

use_cases.title: Coordinate parallel feature work
use_cases.description: Use trajectory tracking and independent commits to reduce conflicts between AI agents working in the same repository.
use_cases.target_user: Engineering teams using AI coding agents

use_cases.title: Improve repeated deployment workflows
use_cases.description: Record deployment steps, outcomes, and critiques so later agents can reuse successful operation patterns.

use_cases.title: Structure AI-assisted code review
use_cases.description: Guide reviewer agents through diff inspection, issue aggregation, and outcome recording for future review suggestions.

prompt_templates.title: Start a tracked task
prompt_templates.prompt: Use agentic-jujutsu to plan a tracked workflow for this task: [task]. Include the trajectory name, safe repository commands, and success criteria.

prompt_templates.title: Review repository status
prompt_templates.prompt: Use agentic-jujutsu patterns to inspect current repository status and propose the next safe version-control step. Do not run mutating commands without approval.

prompt_templates.title: Coordinate multiple agents
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a multi-agent version-control workflow for [feature set]. Assign agent roles, trajectory names, merge checkpoints, and review gates.

prompt_templates.title: Build a learning loop
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a guarded learning loop for repeated [workflow]. Include how to record operations, score outcomes, reject risky suggestions, and improve future recommendations.

output_examples.input: Plan version-control coordination for three AI agents building authentication, billing, and tests.
output_examples.output: A role plan with separate trajectories, branch names, review checkpoints, merge order, and success scoring guidance.

output_examples.input: Assess whether to replay a suggested deployment sequence from prior trajectories.
output_examples.output: A risk-aware recommendation that checks confidence, reviews each operation, requires approval for deployment commands, and records the final outcome.

output_examples.input: Summarize why recent merge attempts failed.
output_examples.output: A plain-language summary of similar trajectories, repeated failure causes, recommended safeguards, and the next low-risk merge step.

best_practices: Review every command before execution, especially pushes, rebases, merges, and deployment operations.
Use clear task descriptions and honest success scores so learned patterns remain useful.
Verify the external package source, version, and permissions before using it in sensitive repositories.
anti_patterns: Do not auto-run AI-recommended operations without human review.
Do not record secrets, tokens, or private incident details in trajectory critiques.
Do not rely on quantum-security or performance claims without validating the installed package.

faq.question: Does this skill replace Git?
faq.answer: No. It provides guidance for using Agentic Jujutsu workflows around version-control tasks and agent coordination.

faq.question: Can it run commands automatically?
faq.answer: The skill shows command execution examples through the external package. Users should require approval before running mutating commands.

faq.question: Is it safe for private repositories?
faq.answer: Use caution. Review the external package, avoid logging secrets, and control access to operation history.

faq.question: What is ReasoningBank used for?
faq.answer: The skill describes ReasoningBank as a way to record task trajectories and suggest future workflows from past outcomes.

faq.question: Does the audit confirm the cryptography claims?
faq.answer: No. The scanned skill only documents those claims. The package implementation must be reviewed separately.

faq.question: Which assistants can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report marks it as compatible with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
